desc: Krong Kep is Cambodia's low-key seafood coast: quieter and more compact than Sihanoukville, and more sea-focused than nearby Kampot, with crab-market dining, short national-park walks, and easy island boat access.
hls: Iconic Kep crab market with Kampot pepper seafood
Relaxed small-town coast with fewer crowds than major Cambodian beach hubs
Quick access to Kep National Park ridge trails and viewpoints
Easy twin-base pairing with Kampot river town stays

todo.name: Kep crab market lunch
todo.desc: Order fresh blue crab with Kampot pepper sauce at the iconic waterfront stalls.

todo.name: Kep National Park perimeter loop
todo.desc: Start early for cooler shaded climbing and Gulf of Thailand lookouts.

todo.name: Rabbit Island day boat
todo.desc: Take a morning boat for simple beach time and rustic seafood shacks.

todo.name: Kampot pepper farm visit
todo.desc: Tour a certified farm to understand cultivation and tasting grades.

todo.name: Abandoned villa hillside circuit
todo.desc: Explore Kep's less-obvious French-era villa ruins for architectural and war-era context.

todo.name: Phnom Chhngok cave temple side trip
todo.desc: Add this high-value half-day excursion near Kampot for a pre-Angkorian brick shrine in a limestone cave.

wifi.note: Adequate in most guesthouses; backup mobile data recommended.

prac.fltNote: There are no nonstop UK/Ireland flights to Kep: most routes are one-stop to Phnom Penh (PNH) via hubs such as Singapore, Bangkok, Doha, or Kuala Lumpur, then a 3-4 hour private transfer/bus; some itineraries use Sihanoukville (KOS) plus a shorter road leg, with Dec-Feb and UK school-holiday weeks raising long-haul and regional fares.

airports.name: Phu Quoc International Airport (Vietnam, border transfer)

prac.currency: Cambodia local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Rabbit Island boats are most likely to pause in rough afternoon seas during the Jun-Sep monsoon.
ATMs are fewer than in Phnom Penh/Siem Reap, so keep mixed small USD and riel for market and boat payments.
Evening road lighting outside central Kep is limited; arrange return transport before late dinners or hill visits.

sweet: Dec-Mar for drier weather and calmer coastal conditions.
